prediction.reasoning: The point estimate for the 3-day price is set to the current price, as short-term predictions without specific catalysts or trends often revert to the current value. The confidence intervals are calculated based on a simplified model that estimates future price movement using an assumed daily volatility. A hypothetical daily standard deviation of 1.5% was used, which translates to a 3-day standard deviation scaled by the square root of time (sqrt(3)). The intervals assume a normal distribution of price changes. This model does not incorporate real-time market data, technical indicators, fundamental analysis, company-specific news, or broader economic factors, all of which can significantly influence stock prices. Stock market predictions are inherently uncertain and speculative.
